title: Refining outputs of generative models
abstract: One example method includes: obtaining, by an artificial intelligence (AI) system and prior to receiving a query, input data; generating, by the AI system and based on the input data, one or more candidate digital components using a machine learning model; obtaining, by the AI system, user preference data limiting usage of at least one candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components; receiving, by the AI system, the query; identifying, by the AI system, one or more digital component regulations associated with the query; identifying, by the AI system based on the one or more digital component regulations and the user preference data, at least one particular candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components; and generating, by the AI system and based on the at least one particular candidate digital component, an output digital component.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A computer-implemented method, comprising: obtaining, by an artificial intelligence (Al) system and prior to receiving a query, input data; generating, by the Al system and based on the input data, one or more candidate digital components using a machine learning model; obtaining, by the Al system, user preference data limiting usage of at least one candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components; receiving, by the Al system, the query: identifying, by the Al system, one or more digital component regulations associated with the query; identifying, by the Al system based on the one or more digital component regulations and the user preference data, at least one particular candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components; and generating, by the Al system and based on the at least one particular candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components, an output digital component. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein generating, by the Al system and based on the input data, the one or more candidate digital components using the machine learning model comprises: generating, by the Al system, a prompt comprising the input data; inputting, by the Al system, the prompt into the machine learning model; and generating, by the machine learning model, the one or more candidate digital components. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, wherein generating, by the Al system, the prompt comprising the input data comprises: obtaining, by the Al system, additional input data comprising data different from the input data, wherein the additional input data limits digital components generated by the machine learning model; and generating, by the Al system, the prompt comprising the input data and the additional input data. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the user preference data indicates at least one of a serving time period, a geographical location, or an event for using the at least one candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, comprising, before receiving the query: obtaining, by the Al system, one or more basic regulation review results associated with the one or more candidate digital components, wherein each basic regulation review result indicates whether a corresponding candidate digital component violates a basic digital component regulation. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, wherein obtaining, by the Al system, the one or more basic regulation review results associated with the one or more candidate digital components comprises: inputting a candidate digital component and one or more basic digital component regulations into an additional machine learning model to determine whether the candidate digital component violates at least one of the one or more basic digital component regulations. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, comprising: determining, by the Al system, that a basic regulation review result indicates that a candidate digital component violates a basic digital component regulation; and in response to determining that the basic regulation review result indicates that the candidate digital component violates the basic digital component regulation, removing the candidate digital component from the one or more candidate digital components. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein identifying, by the Al system based on the one or more digital component regulations and the user preference data, the at least one particular candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components comprises: determining whether a candidate digital component complies with the one or more digital component regulations and the user preference data. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein determining whether the candidate digital component complies with the one or more digital component regulations and the user preference data comprises: inputting the candidate digital component, the one or more digital component regulations, and the user preference data into an additional machine learning model to determine whether the candidate digital component complies with the one or more digital component regulations and the user preference data. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, comprising: obtaining, by the Al system, performance data indicating an acceptance level of each candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components, wherein identifying, by the Al system based on the one or more digital component regulations and the user preference data, the at least one particular candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components comprises: identifying, by the Al system based on the one or more digital component regulations, the user preference data, and the performance data, the at least one particular candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components. 11. A computer-implemented method, comprising: providing, by an artificial intelligence (Al) system, a plurality of levels of automation comprising an automatic mode; receiving, by the Al system, a first query indicating the automatic mode, the first query comprising a first objective; generating, by the Al system using a generative model and based on the first query, one or more first tasks for achieving the first objective; and automatically executing, by the Al system, the one or more first tasks. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 11, wherein the plurality of levels of automation comprise a semi-automatic mode, and wherein the computer- implemented method comprises: receiving, by the Al sy stem, a second query indicating the semi-automatic mode, the second query comprising a second objective; 

 generating, by the Al system using the generative model and based on the second query, one or more second tasks for achieving the second objective; transmitting, by the Al system to a client device, the one or more second tasks; receiving, by the Al system from the client device, an approval of the one or more second tasks; and executing, by the Al system, the one or more second tasks. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 1, wherein generating, by the Al system using the generative model and based on the first query, the one or more first tasks for achieving the first objective comprises: generating, by the Al system, a prompt comprising the first query; inputting, by the Al system, the prompt into the generative model; and generating, by the generative model, the one or more first tasks. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 11. comprising: generating, by the Al system, a prioritized task list indicating a sequence of executing the one or more first tasks, wherein automatically executing, by the Al system, the one or more first tasks comprises: automatically executing, by the Al system, the one or more first tasks based on the sequence of executing the one or more first tasks. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 11, comprising: identifying, by the Al system, performance data associated with executing the one or more first tasks; generating, by the Al system, training data based on the performance data; and refining, by the Al system, the generative model using the training data. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 15, wherein the performance data comprises at least one of clickthrough rate (CTR), conversion rate (CVR), or cost per day (CPD). 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 11. comprising: 

 generating, by the Al system, a summary based on execution results associated with the one or more first tasks; and transmitting, by the Al system, the summary to a client device. 18. A computer-implemented artificial intelligence (Al) system comprising: one or more processors; and one or more storage devices storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to perform operations comprising: obtaining, by an artificial intelligence (Al) system and prior to receiving a query, input data; generating, by the Al system and based on the input data, one or more candidate digital components using a machine learning model; obtaining, by the Al system, user preference data limiting usage of at least one candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components; receiving, by the Al system, the query; identifying, by the Al system, one or more digital component regulations associated with the query; identifying, by the Al system based on the one or more digital component regulations and the user preference data, at least one particular candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components; and generating, by the Al system and based on the at least one particular candidate digital component of the one or more candidate digital components, an output digital component. 19. The system of claim 18, wherein generating, by the Al system and based on the input data, the one or more candidate digital components using the machine learning model comprises: generating, by the Al system, a prompt comprising the input data; inputting, by the Al system, the prompt into the machine learning model; and generating, by the machine learning model, the one or more candidate digital components. 20. The system of claim 19, wherein generating, by the Al system, the prompt comprising the input data comprises: 

 obtaining, by the Al system, additional input data comprising data different from the input data, wherein the additional input data limits digital components generated by the machine learning model; and generating, by the Al system, the prompt comprising the input data and the additional input data.
